LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (CONSPIRACY-FRAUD) AND 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (CONSPIRACY-CONVERSION) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO PROVIDE SPECIFIC FACTS REGARDING THE REPRESENTATIONS AND THE PROPERTY THAT WAS CONVERTED. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT DEFENDANTS' ASSERTION THAT ALL CO-CONSPIRATORS HAVE TO BE MADE A PARTY TO A CLAIM OF CONSPIRACY. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FURTHER FACTS TO SHOW THESE DEFENDANTS HAD A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO PLAINTIFF AND THAT THIS DUTY WAS BREACHED. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THE DEFENDANTS COMMITTED WRONGS PRIOR TO PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED INJURY TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST AS A REMEDY; NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Plaintiff'S Motion Set Aside Judgment And To Permit Additional Discovery
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George E. Akwo, of GA Law Group, Tel. 310.435.9406, for plaintiffs;
Cheryl A. Noll, of Bowles & Verna LLP, Tel. 925.935.3300, for defendants, appears via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff'S Motion Set Aside Judgment And To Permit Additional Discovery is denied. Relief on the ground of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect" must be shown to have been excusable under the circumstances under CCP Sec. 473(b). Mere negligence in failing to plead the case properly, or to produce sufficient evidence to oppose the motion, does not by itself warrant relief. [See Cochran v. Linn (1984) 159 CA3d 245, 251.] Here, Plaintiff's neglect is not excusable because Plaintiff was given notice on multiple occasions that judgment had been entered, however, Plaintiff and has waited months before taking any action. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.
